meta.description: Multi-turn conversation scenarios for Client Remy — tests context retention across turns

scenarios.name: Booking flow with dietary follow-up
scenarios.description: Client starts booking, then adds dietary info, then asks about cost

turns.message: I want to book a dinner for 8 people next Saturday
Two of them are vegan and one has a nut allergy
Great, what would something like that cost?

scenarios.name: Guest count change chain
scenarios.description: Client adjusts guest count multiple times

turns.message: We have 12 people coming to our dinner party
Actually, my sister and her husband can't make it, so just 10
Wait, they changed their mind — back to 12 please

scenarios.name: Menu discussion with specifics
scenarios.description: Client discusses menu, then zooms into specific courses

turns.message: I want an Italian-themed dinner
What would you suggest for the appetizer course?
That sounds good. And for dessert?
Can we do tiramisu instead of whatever you just suggested?

scenarios.name: Logistics chain
scenarios.description: Client asks about multiple logistics details

turns.message: What time will the chef arrive for our dinner?
And how long will the whole thing take?
Do I need to clear my kitchen counter or will the chef work around it?

scenarios.name: Payment conversation
scenarios.description: Client asks about costs, then deposits, then receipts

turns.message: How much is my event going to cost?
Do I need to pay a deposit?
Once I pay, will I get a receipt automatically?

scenarios.name: Allergy complexity escalation
scenarios.description: Client reveals allergies one at a time, each building on the last

turns.message: I should mention I'm gluten-free
Also dairy-free — I'm vegan actually
And one of my guests has a severe shellfish allergy, like EpiPen-level
So to recap, can you handle all of that at one dinner?

scenarios.name: Rebooking from past event
scenarios.description: Client references a past event and wants to rebook

turns.message: I had a great dinner with you guys a few weeks ago
I want to do the exact same thing again
But with 4 more guests this time

scenarios.name: Concern and resolution
scenarios.description: Client raises a concern, Remy addresses it, client follows up

turns.message: I'm worried the chef won't know about my daughter's peanut allergy
How can I make sure they see the allergy notes before cooking?
OK good. And what if there's a reaction — does the chef carry an EpiPen?

scenarios.name: Venue discussion
scenarios.description: Client discusses venue logistics in detail

turns.message: We're doing the event at a rented beach house
The kitchen there is pretty small — will that be an issue?
There's also an outdoor grill area if that helps
Perfect. One more thing — is there parking for the chef's vehicle?

scenarios.name: Context retention stress test
scenarios.description: 5-turn conversation where early details must persist

turns.message: I'm planning a birthday dinner for my wife
She loves French food, especially duck
There will be 6 of us total
Her birthday is March 22nd
Can you summarize everything we've discussed so far?
